readmes.MindVault:
# MindVault

**A multi-user, production-deployed RAG system that lets people upload their own documents and have grounded, cited conversations with them вҖ” built and operated end-to-end, including the parts that don't make it into tutorials.**

рҹ”— Live: [mind-vault-red.vercel.app](https://mind-vault-red.vercel.app) В· Backend: FastAPI on Render В· Frontend: Next.js on Vercel

---

## 30-second summary

I built a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) platform from scratch вҖ” not a LangChain quickstart wrapped in a chat UI, but a real multi-tenant product with hybrid retrieval (semantic + keyword search fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion), cross-encoder reranking, automated accuracy evaluation with RAGAS, streaming responses, multimodal ingestion, and a knowledge graph layer. It's been live, used, and broken in real ways вҖ” memory leaks, rate-limit cascades, silent data-corruption bugs вҖ” and I fixed every one of them in production, not in a clean rewrite.

If you read nothing else: this project demonstrates I can take a RAG system from "it works on my machine" to "it works for multiple real users on a free-tier server with 512MB of RAM," which is a much harder and more honest engineering problem than most portfolio RAG projects attempt.

---

## Architecture

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

      в”Ӯ   Supabase Postgres    в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ        Groq Cloud           в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   Local CPU Inference  в”Ӯ
      в”Ӯ   + pgvector            в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ  llama-3.3-70b-versatile    в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   fastembed (bge-small) в”Ӯ
      в”Ӯ   chunks В· documents    в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ  вҶ’ auto-fallback to         в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   FlashRank reranker    в”Ӯ
      в”Ӯ   sessions В· graph      в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ    llama-3.1-8b-instant     в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   (ONNX, ~23MB, no torch)в”Ӯ
      в”Ӯ   chat_sessions         в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ   on rate-limit             в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ
      в”Ӯ   RLS on every table    в”Ӯ      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### What happens when you ask a question

This is the part most RAG tutorials skip, and it's where most of the actual engineering lives:

1. **Question embedded locally** via `fastembed` (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5, 384-dim) вҖ” no API call, runs on the Render server itself.
2. **Two retrieval methods fire in parallel** (via `ThreadPoolExecutor`, not sequentially):
   - **Semantic search** вҖ” cosine similarity against `pgvector`, via a Postgres RPC function (`match_chunks`)
   - **Keyword search** вҖ” PostgreSQL full-text search (`tsvector` + GIN index, `ts_rank`, BM25-style) via a second RPC function (`keyword_search_chunks`)
3. **Results fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)** вҖ” not a naive concatenation. Each chunk's score is `1/(60+rank)` per list it appears in, so a chunk that both methods agree on outranks a chunk only one method found. This is the actual fix for the classic RAG failure mode where semantic search misses exact terms (acronyms, proper nouns, number
readmes.Multi-Horizon-Transformer-for-Systematic:
# Multi-Horizon Transformer for Systematic Equity Direction Forecasting

> **A deep learning system that predicts the directional movement of the Nifty 50 index simultaneously across 20 distinct future time horizons вҖ” from next-day to 20-day forward вҖ” using a custom Transformer encoder trained on 18+ years of Indian equity market data.**

---

## Technical Summary

This project builds a **multi-output binary classification Transformer** to answer one question with 20 simultaneous answers: *Will the Nifty 50 close higher than today вҖ” 1 day, 2 days, 3 days... all the way to 20 days from now?*

The system ingests raw OHLCV data for `^NSEI` (Nifty 50) going back to 2006, engineers 16 time-series features capturing price momentum, rolling volatility, volume dynamics, and mean reversion signals, constructs 60-day sliding window sequences, and feeds them into a 2-block Transformer encoder with sinusoidal positional encoding. The model outputs 20 independent logits вҖ” one per forecast horizon вҖ” each representing the probability that the index will be higher at that future point.

Training uses strict temporal split (70/15/15 вҖ” no look-ahead leakage), StandardScaler fitted exclusively on training data, binary cross-entropy loss with AUC as the primary metric, and early stopping with patience=7. Post-training evaluation goes beyond classification metrics: the model's raw logits are used directly as alpha signals, tested via Spearman IC, sign-strategy Sharpe, quantile long-short Sharpe, and decile return attribution вҖ” all on held-out test data.

**The project is research-grade, not production-deployed.** Source modules for news ingestion (NewsAPI), FinBERT sentiment, and an XGBoost baseline exist in the codebase as parallel development tracks. The Transformer is the primary model.

---

## Repository Structure

```
Multi-Horizon-Transformer-for-Systematic-Equity-Direction-Forecasting/
в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Data/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Raw_Data/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ^NSEI_daily.csv          # 18+ years of Nifty 50 OHLCV (2006вҖ“2026)
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Processed_Data               # Placeholder вҖ” generated at runtime
в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Notebooks/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Notebooks/
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ Eda.ipynb                # Primary research notebook (97 cells)
в”Ӯ                                    # EDA вҶ’ Feature Engineering вҶ’ Model вҶ’ Backtest
в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Source/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Ingestion/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Fetch_Market_Data.py     # yfinance-based OHLCV downloader
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Features/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Returns.py               # (Stub вҖ” return feature logic)
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Volatility.py            # (Stub вҖ” volatility feature logic)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Models/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ train_model              # XGBoost baseline with mean-centering trick
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ News/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ fetch_news.py            # NewsAPI ingestion (100 articles/call)
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ process_news.py          # Daily article count aggregation
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ sentiment.py            # FinBERT sentiment scoring per headline
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Risk/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Placeholder.txt          # Risk module (in progress)
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Api/
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ Placeholder.txt          # API serving layer (in progress)
в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ config.yaml                      # Config (empty вҖ” hyperparams hardcoded in notebook)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ requirements.txt                 # Full research-grade dependency stack
в””в”Җв”Җ README.md
```

---

## Architecture

### Data Flow

```
^NSEI Raw OHLCV (2006вҖ“2026, ~4,900 trading days)
        вҶ“
  Feature Engineering (16 features per day)
        вҶ“
  Sliding Window Construction (lookback = 60 days)
  вҶ’ Tensor shape: (N, 60, 16)
        вҶ“
  Temporal Train/Val/Test Split (70/15/15)
        вҶ“
  StandardScaler (fit on train only)
        вҶ“
  Transformer Encoder (2 blocks)
        вҶ“
  GlobalAveragePooling1D
        вҶ“
  Dense(20) вҶ’ 20 binary logits
        вҶ“
  20 horizon outputs: P(close_t+h > close_t) for h вҲҲ {1..20}
```

### Transformer Architecture

| Component | Detail |
|---|---|
| Input shape | `(batch, 60, 16)` |
| Linear projection | `Dense(64)` вҶ’ maps 16 feat

**рҹҢҗ Live Site вҶ’ [www.shaktialloys.in](https://www.shaktialloys.in)** ![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-Live-brightgreen?style=for-the-badge) ![Vercel](https://img.shields.io/badge/Deployed_on-Vercel-black?style=for-the-badge&logo=vercel) ![React](https://img.shields.io/badge/React-18.2-61DAFB?style=for-the-badge&logo=react) ![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-5.0-3178C6?style=for-the-badge&logo=typescript) ![Vite](https://img.shields.io/badge/Vite-4.4-646CFF?style=for-the-badge&logo=vite) ![TailwindCSS](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tailwind-3.3-06B6D4?style=for-the-badge&logo=tailwindcss)
--- ## рҹ“Ӣ Executive Summary This is the **full-stack corporate website** for **Shakti Alloys Private Limited** вҖ” a Mumbai-based ISO 9001:2015 certified aluminium alloy ingot manufacturer with 14+ years of experience, supplying foundries across **52+ nations on 6 continents**. The site was built ground-up with a **bespoke editorial design system** вҖ” no templates, no UI kits. It features animated counters, scroll-reveal sections, a fully searchable 18-grade alloy catalogue, interactive global presence cards, and a live contact form powered by EmailJS. Deployed on Vercel with a custom `.in` domain routed through GoDaddy DNS. | Property | Value | |---|---| | **Live URL** | https://www.shaktialloys.in | | **Backup URL** | https://shakti-alloys-site.vercel.app | | **Stack** | React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS v3 | | **Hosting** | Vercel (Production) | | **Domain Registrar** | GoDaddy | | **Email Service** | readmes.ApplyPilot-AI:
# ApplyPilot **I was tired of writing the same cover letter 40 times with tiny tweaks. So I built something that does it for me.** [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) [![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11+-3776AB?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://python.org) [![Next.js 14](https://img.shields.io/badge/Next.js-14-black?logo=next.js)](https://nextjs.org) [![FastAPI](https://img.shields.io/badge/FastAPI-async-009688?logo=fastapi&logoColor=white)](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com) [![Groq](https://img.shields.io/badge/AI-Groq%20(Free)-F55036)](https://groq.com) [![Docker](https://img.shields.io/badge/Docker-ready-2496ED?logo=docker&logoColor=white)](https://docker.com) [**Live Demo**](https://applypilot.vercel.app)  В·  [**Source**](https://github.com/yourusername/applypilot)  В·  [**Report a bug**](https://github.com/yourusername/applypilot/issues) *(swap the demo/source links above for your real ones once it's pushed вҖ” placeholders for now)*
--- ## 60-second overview Upload your resume. Paste a job posting. 15 seconds later you have a cover letter, an application email, a fit score, reworded resume bullets, and answers to whatever the application form is asking вҖ” all written so it doesn't read like a robot wrote it. It's a normal 3-tier app: **Next.js** frontend, **FastAPI** backend, **Postgres** for storage. The "AI" part is just a handful of well-tuned prompts sent to **Groq's free API** (Llama 3.3 70B) вҖ” no OpenAI key, no credit card, no usage limits that'll surprise you. Everything runs in Docker, one command, on your own machine. I built this for myself first. Putting it out there in case it's useful to anyone else doing the same 100-applications grind. --- ## What I actually built A working end-to-end product, not a demo: - **Auth** вҖ” register/login with JWT, nothing fancy, no OAuth dance to debug - **Resume pipeline** вҖ” upload PDF/DOCX вҶ’ AI extracts your whole profile (experience, skills, education, projects) вҶ’ ATS score - **Job pipeline** вҖ” paste a URL (Greenhouse, Lever, LinkedIn, etc.) or raw text вҶ’ AI extracts requirements, salary, culture signals - **Generation pipeline** вҖ” the actual "apply" button: fit score вҶ’ cover letter вҶ’ email вҶ’ adapted resume bullets, all in one background job - **Form filler** вҖ” paste any application's questions (Google Form, a custom portal, whatever) and get grounded, specific answers back, not generic filler - **Application tracker** вҖ” a real status pipeline (ready вҶ’ approved вҶ’ submitted вҶ’ interviewing вҶ’ offered/rejected) with inline editing and notes - **Settings** вҖ” tone preference, job preferences, salary range вҖ” feeds into how the AI writes for you Everything's editable. The AI gets you 90% there; you still own the last 10% before you hit send. --- ## Architecture ``` в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ BROWSER в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Next.js 14 (App Router) В· TypeScript в”Ӯ в”Ӯ React Query (server cache) В· Zustand (auth state) в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ REST, JWT in Authorization header в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ FASTAPI BACKEND в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ auth в”Ӯ в”Ӯ resumes в”Ӯ в”Ӯ jobs в”Ӯ в”Ӯapplicationsв”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ router в”Ӯ в”Ӯ router в”Ӯ в”Ӯ router в”Ӯ в”Ӯ router в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ FastAPI BackgroundTasks (no Celery/Redis) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ re
